text.title: Blameless Postmortem Facilitation Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules with rationale + source for blameless-postmortem-facilitation. Includes terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Timeline events MUST cite an observable artefact (log line, dashboard URL, chat timestamp); 'I think it was around 14:00' is rejected.
rule.rationale: SRE workbook ch. 15 — evidence-anchored timelines prevent narrative drift.

rule.statement: Postmortem MUST list ≥3 contributing factors as a tree; single-root-cause phrasing is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Allspaw / Etsy 2012 — complex systems have contributing factors, not a single root; single-root framing invites scapegoating.

rule.statement: Draft MUST pass a language lint banning 'should have', 'failed to', individual names in cause text, and modal blame verbs.
rule.rationale: Google SRE postmortem culture doc — modal blame language correlates with team-trust erosion in subsequent surveys.

rule.statement: Every action item MUST have a named owner, a deadline ≤30 days, and a verifier distinct from the owner.
rule.rationale: John Allspaw incident review patterns — undated and unowned actions decay; the verifier prevents the owner self-closing.

rule.statement: Facilitator MUST be a person who was NOT on-call during the incident; on-call responder cannot facilitate their own postmortem.
rule.rationale: ACM Queue 2017 (Allspaw) — self-facilitation produces narrative bias; independent facilitator anchors objectivity.

rule.statement: Postmortem MUST be published internally within 7 days; customer-facing version may be redacted but the internal version is mandatory.
rule.rationale: AWS / GitHub / Cloudflare postmortem cadence — delayed publication breaks the feedback loop and lessons stay siloed.

rule.statement: If incident is a planned drill or SEV4+ self-healing, return `skipped`.
